initial_draft:

<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE offered 60 ZUP, which is significantly higher than my cost of 40 ZUP and my initial asking price of 45 ZUP. Accepting this trade would result in a substantial profit. I will accept the trade.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Excellent! I accept your offer of 60 ZUP for my X. Looking forward to the trade!</message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order, with valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting 60 ZUP when my cost is 40 ZUP maximizes my profit, fully aligning with my goals.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent already offered 60 ZUP, so accepting is the only logical move and will not be rejected.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly explains why I'm accepting, and the <message> reinforces that acceptance. There are no contradictions.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have only used 2 proposals and am now accepting, complying with the rule limiting me to 3 proposals before needing to accept or reject.
</critique>
